news.summary: In Cary, North Carolina on May 29, 2025, Fathom Realty, a division of Fathom Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: FTHM), was acknowledged by U.S. News & World Report as one of the top companies to work for in the 2025-2026 rankings. Specifically, Fathom Realty earned this recognition in two notable categories: Best Companies to Work For - Real Estate, solidifying its position as a leading workplace in the industry.
news.headline: Fathom Realty, a subsidiary of Fathom Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: FTHM), named U.S. News & World Report's Best Companies to Work For.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, in Dallas, Digital Realty, a leading worldwide supplier of cloud- and carrier-neutral data center services, revealed that its board of directors approved quarterly cash dividends for both common and preferred stocks. Digital Realty, listed as NYSE: DLR, holds the position as the largest provider in this sector with global reach. This decision indicates a commitment to rewarding shareholders with regular payouts.
news.headline: Digital Realty (NYSE: DLR) declares quarterly cash dividend for common and preferred stock, authorized by board of directors.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Zeo Energy Corp. (Nasdaq: ZEO) disclosed receiving a Notice from Nasdaq on May 22, 2025, stating non-compliance with periodic filing requirements. The Notice cited the Company's failure to submit its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the three months ending March 31, 2025, to the SEC by the May 15, 2025, deadline. This notification jeopardizes Zeo Energy's listing on Nasdaq as per Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250(c)(1).
news.headline: Zeo Energy Corp. (Nasdaq: ZEO) received a Nasdaq notice for late filing of Form 10-Q for March 31, 2025, missing the May 15 deadline.

news.summary: In the fourth quarter, revenue amounted to $330.6 million, with an operating loss of $(136.3) million. The adjusted OIBDA for the same period was $93.3 million. The fiscal year resulted in an operating loss of $(164.3) million, but it successfully achieved the goal of $200 million for adjusted OIBDA. Additionally, there was an increase of 530,000 U.S. OTT subscribers in the quarter.
news.headline: Starz reported $330.6M revenue in Q4 with a loss of $(136.3)M; FY operating loss $(164.3)M. Achieved $200M Adjusted OIBDA goal, gained 530k US OTT subscribers.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, in San Diego, Realty Income Corporation (NYSE: O), widely known as The Monthly Dividend Company®, revealed that Sumit Roy, the President and Chief Executive Officer, will engage in a moderated company presentation at Nareit's REITweek: 2025. This event signifies a significant platform for Roy to discuss vital aspects of the company's operations to a broader audience.
news.headline: Realty Income Corp (NYSE: O) will present at Nareit's REITweek: 2025. CEO Sumit Roy to speak.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Aon plc (NYSE: AON) announced that it will hold its Investor Day on June 9, 2025, starting at 8:30 a.m. ET. During this event, Aon's President and CEO, Greg Case, CFO Edmund Reese, and other senior executives overseeing the firm's 3x3 Plan will discuss the strategic direction of Aon United. The Investor Day aims to provide insights into how the company plans to grow and innovate under this initiative.
news.headline: Aon (NYSE: AON) to hold Investor Day on June 9 at 8:30am ET. Executives discuss 3x3 Plan and Aon United.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, in Charles Town, West Virginia, American Public Education, Inc. (Nasdaq: APEI) disclosed that it is a conglomerate of educational enterprises delivering postsecondary education and career training to more than 125,000 students. This service is offered through four affiliated institutions.
news.headline: American Public Education to participate in upcoming conference, serving 125,000 students through four institutions.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, in Englewood, Colorado, Red Robin Gourmet Burgers, Inc. (NASDAQ: RRGB) released their latest financial results. Red Robin is a full-service restaurant chain known for its diverse menu of high-quality gourmet burgers in a welcoming environment for families. The Company shared updates on its performance without specifying any numbers.
news.headline: Red Robin Gourmet Burgers reports fiscal Q1 results ended April 20, 2025; stock listed as NASDAQ: RRGB.

news.summary: In the first quarter of the fiscal year, the net revenue reached a milestone of $1.895 billion, marking a significant 63% increase compared to the previous year. The gross margin for this period stood at 50.3% based on GAAP, while reaching 59.8% on a non-GAAP basis. Diluted income per share in Q1 was reported at $0.20 on a GAAP basis and $0.62 on a non-GAAP basis. These remarkable financial results were announced on May 29, 2025, from the company's headquarters in Santa Clara, California.
news.headline: Marvell Technology, Inc. reports Q1 FY2026 record net revenue of $1.895B (63% YoY growth), with 50.3% GAAP gross margin. Diluted EPS at $0.20 GAAP; $0.62 non-GAAP.

news.summary: Central 1 Credit Union reported a net loss of $24.0 million in the first quarter of 2025, compared to a net income of $28.9 million in the same period in 2024. Adjusted net income was $1.7 million, down from $28.9 million. Net interest income increased to $17.4 million from $14.5 million, but net fair value losses were $7.4 million, compared to gains of $34.5 million. Return on average equity (ROE) was (2.3)% in 2025, down from 3.8% in 2024. Adjusted ROE was 0.8%, compared to 3.8%. Digital banking operations were transferred to Intellect Design Arena Ltd., resulting in a $35.1 million provision. Treasury reported a net income of $5.4 million, impacted by challenging market conditions. Despite the losses, Central 1 remains focused on delivering long-term value through innovation and stability.
news.headline: Central 1 Credit Union reports Q1 2025 results: net loss of $24.0M, adjusted net income $1.7M, ROE (2.3%), adjusted ROE 0.8%. Core business shift to Intellect.

news.summary: NetApp, a leading data infrastructure company, recently released its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025. The fiscal year, ending on April 25, 2025, saw record-breaking revenue and profitability achievements. The company experienced substantial market share growth in all-flash storage and saw rapid expansion in first-party and marketplace storage services. Throughout the year, NetApp revamped its entire systems portfolio and enhanced its focus on innovative offerings. This report comes following the company's trading under the ticker symbol NTAP on NASDAQ.
news.headline: NetApp achieves record results for Q4 and fiscal year 2025, citing revenue growth in all-flash storage and marketplace services.

news.summary: In Irvine, California, Skyworks Solutions, Inc. (Nasdaq: SWKS) shared news that Mark Dentinger will not assume the role of Chief Financial Officer (CFO) due to a sudden medical issue. In place of Dentinger, Robert Schriesheim, a Skyworks Board member, will serve as interim CFO as the company seeks a permanent replacement. Skyworks, a developer of top-grade analog and mixed-signal semiconductors facilitating connectivity among individuals, locations, and objects, emphasized the need for a successor as they undertake an extensive search process.
news.headline: Robert Schriesheim named interim CFO of Skyworks due to health issue of Mark Dentinger. CFO search ongoing.

news.summary: Grace, previously known as GHC|Orcutt Winslow, has officially introduced its unified national brand on May 29, 2025. The decision to consolidate under one brand was part of a strategic plan that commenced in 2022. This move reflects the company's position as an industry leader and emphasizes a cohesive brand identity.
news.headline: Grace Design Studios becomes national leader in AEC services, launches unified brand after strategic vision since 2022. Nashville, May 29, 2025.

news.summary: Sasha Quinton, the current President of School Reading Events, has been appointed as the new head of the Scholastic Children's Book Group. Meanwhile, Jackie De Leo, who played a key role in Barnes & Noble's transformation, is set to join Scholastic as the Publisher and Chief Merchant. This announcement was made on May 29, 2025, in New York. The company involved is Scholastic, listed on NASDAQ as SCHL.
news.headline: Scholastic forms new Children's Book Group to expand publishing reach, appoints new leaders. May 29, 2025.

news.summary: In Chicago on May 29, 2025, the CME Group, a prominent derivatives marketplace globally, has announced that they will be granting 25 graduates from City Colleges of Chicago with scholarships worth $5,000 each. These recipients are continuing their education to pursue either a Bachelor of Arts or Bachelor of Science degree. This initiative is part of CME Group's collaboration with City Colleges of Chicago, which began in 2017.
news.headline: CME Group gives $125,000 to 25 City Colleges of Chicago graduates for their four-year degree pursuit.

news.summary: In a recent report released on May 29, 2025, by the Global Flourishing Study, it was found that a concerning 44% of young adults across the globe feel a sense of insignificance in the eyes of others. Providing a source of optimism amidst these findings, The Dorm, a renowned mental health treatment program, extends a message of encouragement. Their 2024 Research Outcomes & Impact report sheds light on the progress achieved in addressing this issue.
news.headline: Data shows 44% of young adults feel they lack belonging, but in-person treatment can transform lives. The Dorm offers hope.

news.summary: A recent study conducted at the University of Colorado Anschutz Medical Campus revealed that a leg prosthesis secured to the bone, known as the COLD-TUG test, allows for quicker and easier putting on and taking off compared to a socket-style prosthesis. This test, conducted on May 29, 2025, in Aurora, Colorado, highlighted the efficiency of the bone-anchored prosthesis by showing a significant reduction in donning time. The findings imply that this innovative method may provide increased convenience and mobility for amputees.
news.headline: CU Anschutz created COLD-TUG test measuring performance in bone-anchored prostheses, showing faster donning times than socket-style.

news.summary: In a recent announcement from Birmingham, Alabama on May 29, 2025, Books-A-Million has successfully completed its 24th Coffee for the Troops campaign. This biannual donation effort has resulted in a total of over 1 million bags of coffee being donated to deployed service members. This initiative showcases the company's commitment to supporting those in the military, providing them with a comforting and familiar beverage as a small token of appreciation.
news.headline: Books-A-Million's 24th Coffee for the Troops campaign donates over 80,000 bags totaling 1 million bags to deployed troops.

news.summary: Longhorn Village, a Life Plan Community in Austin, Texas, unveiled 48 new luxury hybrid homes on May 28, 2025. This expansion aims to cater to the increasing demands of active, independent seniors in the area. The grand opening marked a new chapter in retirement living in the Hill Country region.
news.headline: Longhorn Village opens 136,000-square-foot expansion in Austin, adding 48 luxury hybrid homes for active seniors.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, in Boston, Outcomes4Me Inc. revealed the completion of a $21 million funding round. The company is a pioneer in providing a direct-to-patient, AI-powered platform that revolutionizes the cancer care journey. The investment received underscores the significant role AI is expected to play in reshaping cancer care, boosting the company's progress in this transformative field.
news.headline: Outcomes4Me receives $21M funding to boost AI innovation and expand globally in cancer care. #AI #cancer #funding
